milestone_brief: Close the deferred work from enriques-taubin-spike-2026q2-e1 Path B: expose the second Taubin pass (n_iter=40, pass_band=0.05) as an OPT-IN slider for Enriques figs 1+2 (double-curve topology). Spike measured median +138ms (+30.8%) overhead — 587.5ms total vs 500ms budget — so it cannot be the default but is valuable for screenshot/publication-quality renders. Architecture decision space the researcher must resolve: (1) Where does the toggle live? Options: (a) per-figure ParamSpec on figs 1+2 (boolean param 'hq_smoothing' that adds to the existing c slider and the lam0/lam3 sliders); (b) global app-level setting via a 'HQ smoothing' checkbox in the Appearance panel that applies to whatever variety is active; (c) global Tools or Settings menu entry. Researcher should evaluate against AI-8 (Surface dataclass frozen — adding a ParamSpec is the established extension point; adding a global app-level setting is a new surface), Pattern-A precedent (enriques-backface-2026q2-e1 used MainWindow gating, not ParamSpec), and discoverability (a panel checkbox is the most user-facing but adds Qt-panel surface; a ParamSpec slider is the lowest-friction code path but less discoverable). (2) Granularity: boolean on/off OR a slider for n_iter? The spike used n_iter=40. (3) Scope: does it apply only when active variety is 'Enriques surface' AND active subtype is fig 1 or fig 2, OR is it a universal 'extra smoothing' lever that any implicit-surface generator could honor? (4) Default state: confirmed OFF at launch to preserve the 449ms baseline. (5) Persistence: out of scope (QSettings is CONTEXT.md §9 non-goal); the toggle resets per-session. Implementation must NOT change the default behavior of enriques_figure_1/2 — they STILL run single-pass at bounds=1.89 by default. The second pass only fires when the new opt-in is True. Test surface: add tests/test_enriques_hq_smoothing.py with (a) generator returns valid mesh when opt-in is True, (b) opt-in=True mesh has lower vertex-normal variance than opt-in=False (quantitative quality guard), (c) opt-in default is False (regression guard against accidentally enabling). AI invariants: AI-6 (smoothing STACKS on existing first pass; do NOT replace), AI-8 (no Surface dataclass field change — use ParamSpec OR Pattern-A state-on-panel), AI-9 (no processEvents — generator-time only), AI-14 (generator contract preserved), AI-2 (Qt-free tests). The realtime-variety-render-e1/e2 clipped-mesh cache helps slider-release responsiveness but NOT initial generate-time; toggling HQ smoothing forces a fresh generate (cache invalidation). Surface the cache-invalidation requirement to the researcher so they propose the right wire-up. Recommended researcher mode: --single (Sonnet). The brief is detailed enough for a single research pass to resolve the architecture question and produce a buildable plan.
research_synthesis: Option (b) AppearancePanel toggle (Pattern-A): _hq_smoothing bool stored on panel; hq_smoothing_changed Signal(bool) emitted on toggle → MainWindow._on_hq_smoothing_changed calls _invalidate_clipped_mesh + _render_current. Per-subtype gating in _on_subtype_changed enables toggle only for Enriques figs 1+2 (HQ_ELIGIBLE_SUBTYPES set). Generator API: hq_smoothing kwarg on figs 1+2 only; _marching_cubes_to_polydata gains second_smooth_iter int kwarg (default 0 preserves all non-Enriques behavior). 7 AI-2 tests via inspect.signature + variance comparison.
